title: Phase F: Create New-ElmWebSocketDriver + Start-ElmWebServer
description: New-ElmWebSocketDriver (Public/Drivers/): Creates InputQueue (ConcurrentQueue[PSCustomObject]) and OutputQueue (ConcurrentQueue[string]). Calls Invoke-ElmWebSocketListener. Returns { InputQueue, OutputSink, OutputQueue, Stop } where OutputSink is a scriptblock that enqueues to OutputQueue. Start-ElmWebServer (Public/Runtime/): Same params as Start-ElmProgram plus -Port (default 8080) and -Width/-Height (defaults 220x50 - no PTY available so cannot use [Console]::WindowWidth). Calls New-ElmWebSocketDriver, then calls Invoke-ElmEventLoop with -OutputSink driver.OutputSink. Does NOT open browser (Start-Process). Handles cleanup on exit. NOTE: must bypass Start-ElmProgram's PTY dimension validation since no console is attached.

title: Phase A: Add -OutputSink to Invoke-ElmEventLoop
description: Invoke-ElmEventLoop calls [Console]::Write directly at 4 sites (hideCursor, initial render, FullRedraw, patch, legacy path, showCursor). Add optional [scriptblock]=null param. When set, all Console::Write calls are replaced with: if (OutputSink) { & OutputSink ansi } else { Console::Write(ansi) }. Start-ElmProgram passes null (unchanged behavior). Start-ElmWebServer passes a scriptblock that enqueues to a ConcurrentQueue.

title: Phase 7: WebSocket + xterm.js browser serving
description: Implement Start-ElmWebServer so any existing Elm app can be served in a browser with zero changes to app code. Architecture: HttpListener serves an xterm.js page, WebSocket bridges keystrokes to the InputQueue and ANSI output back to the browser. See docs/plan/elm-powershell-plan.md Phase 7 section and memories/session/plan.md for full spec.

title: Phase G: Pester tests for all Phase 7 functions
description: Write Pester tests for: ConvertFrom-AnsiVtSequence.Tests.ps1 (VT sequence parsing table-driven), Get-ElmXtermPage.Tests.ps1 (HTML structure, correct port substitution, JS assignments not method calls), Invoke-ElmWebSocketListener.Tests.ps1 (mock HttpListener, queue plumbing), New-ElmWebSocketDriver.Tests.ps1 (driver shape), Start-ElmWebServer.Tests.ps1 (integration). Also regression test for Invoke-ElmEventLoop with -OutputSink.

title: Phase E: Create Invoke-ElmWebSocketListener
description: New private function at Private/Drivers/Invoke-ElmWebSocketListener.ps1. Runs in a background runspace. Creates HttpListener on http://localhost:{port}/. Accept loop: GetContext() -> check IsWebSocketRequest (NOT url path alone). GET non-WS requests: serve HTML page with no-cache headers. WS request: if connection active return 409; else AcceptWebSocketAsync('elm-tui').GetAwaiter().GetResult(). Receive runspace: ReceiveAsync loop -> UTF8 decode -> ConvertFrom-AnsiVtSequence -> InputQueue.Enqueue (each item). Send runspace: drains OutputQueue (ConcurrentQueue[string]) -> SendAsync. WebSocket context must be swappable on reconnect. Parameters: -Port, -InputQueue, -OutputQueue, -HtmlContent.

title: Phase D: Create Get-ElmXtermPage HTML generator
description: New private function at Private/Web/Get-ElmXtermPage.ps1. Accepts -Port [int]. Returns self-contained HTML string with: inline xterm.min.js (from \), inline xterm-addon-fit.min.js, Terminal init with convertEol:true and FitAddon, WebSocket connecting to ws://localhost:{port}/ws, term.onData(d => ws.send(d)) for input, ws.onmessage = (e) => term.write(e.data) for output (NOTE: assignment not method call), window resize handler sending ESC[8;rows;colst, ws.onclose reconnect loop with setTimeout.

title: Phase C: Bundle xterm.js v5.5.0 in Private/Web/
description: Download xterm.min.js and xterm-addon-fit.min.js (v5.5.0) from unpkg and save to Private/Web/. Add to Elm.psm1 at module load: \ = Get-Content \\/Private/Web/xterm.min.js\ -Raw and \ = Get-Content \\/Private/Web/xterm-addon-fit.min.js\ -Raw. Files are ~350KB + ~5KB. Air-gap safe - no CDN dependency at runtime.

title: Phase B: Create ConvertFrom-AnsiVtSequence
description: New private function at Private/Web/ConvertFrom-AnsiVtSequence.ps1. Takes raw VT string from xterm.js onData, returns one or more PSCustomObject matching existing InputQueue format: { Type=KeyDown, Key=[ConsoleKey], Char=[char], Modifiers=[ConsoleModifiers] }. Must handle: printable chars, control chars (Ctrl+A-Z), Escape, Backspace (0x7f), Enter (0x0d), Tab, VT arrow/nav sequences (ESC[A/B/C/D, ESC[H/F, ESC[5~/6~, etc.), modified sequences (ESC[1;5A = Ctrl+UpArrow). Also handles Resize: ESC[8;rows;colst -> { Type=Resize, Width, Height }. IMPORTANT: plan doc says canonical strings but real impl uses PSCustomObjects - must match PSCustomObjects.

title: Remove all em-dashes from codebase for PS5.1 compatibility
description: PS5.1 reads UTF-8 files as CP1252, where the 0x94 byte of an em-dash (U+2014, UTF-8: E2 80 94) is decoded as a right double-quote, breaking string termination and causing cascading parse errors. All em-dashes (and en-dashes) must be replaced with ASCII hyphens across the entire codebase including source, docs, plans, ADRs, and READMEs.

title: bug: Replace patch stale chars when new content shorter than old
description: When a text node's content shrinks (e.g. 'Focus: Right' -> 'Focus: Left'), Replace patch Width = newLeaf.Width = 11. Trailer calc uses patchWidth=11 so 11-11=0 trailing spaces. The stale 't' at position 12 is never overwritten. Fix: include OldWidth = oldLeaf.Width in Replace patch; use max(Width,OldWidth) as clearWidth for the trailing space region.
close_reason: Fixed via OldWidth/clearWidth/trailer pattern in ConvertTo-AnsiPatch and OldWidth in Compare-ElmViewTree. Committed b475511.

title: bug: ConvertTo-AnsiPatch ESC[K erases right-side content in horizontal layouts
description: Replace patches emit ESC[K (erase to EOL) before writing content. In horizontal row layouts (e.g. ComponentDemo), updating the left component erases right component content on the same terminal row. Fix: remove ESC[K and instead pad content to exact Width with spaces.

title: Rename module from Elm to PSTea with Tea command prefix
description: Rename module files (Elm.psd1/psm1 -> PSTea.psd1/psm1), all public/private functions (*-Elm* -> *-Tea*), test files, example scripts, and README. Add ADR-025 and plan doc. Rationale: Elm is a programming language; this module implements the architecture (TEA), not the language. See docs/adr/ADR-025-module-rename-elm-to-pstea.md.

title: Correct plan doc Phase 7 inaccuracies
description: Fix docs/plan/elm-powershell-plan.md Phase 7 section to correct: (1) ConcurrentQueue[string] -> ConcurrentQueue[PSCustomObject] for input; (2) OutputQueue on event loop -> OutputSink scriptblock; (3) JS onmessage/onclose assignment syntax; (4) note resize deferred; (5) note Elm.psm1 must be updated for JS loading; (6) note Start-ElmWebServer does NOT open browser automatically; (7) document 4 Console::Write sites not 3.

title: Write ADRs for Phase 7 design decisions
description: Write ADR-021 through ADR-024 covering: ADR-021: OutputSink scriptblock on Invoke-ElmEventLoop (not OutputQueue param; all Console::Write sites routed through it). ADR-022: WebSocket driver input format (PSCustomObjects not canonical strings; plan doc inaccuracy corrected). ADR-023: Start-ElmWebServer default dimensions (220x50 fixed; no PTY; bypasses Start-ElmProgram validation). ADR-024: Single-connection WebSocket model with 409 on concurrent attempt.

title: Widget showcase: number keys jump to panel, split Numeric/Dots pager state
description: Two UX improvements to Invoke-WidgetShowcaseDemo.ps1: (1) Tab labels show [1]-[7] but pressing the number key does nothing - wire D1-D7 key subs to jump directly to that panel. (2) Numeric and Dots paginators share PagerPage, so navigating one moves the other - add PagerDotsPage to model and split their state.

title: feat: five new example demos (Stopwatch, Quiz, ColorPicker, FileExplorer, Snake)
description: Add five interactive demo scripts to Examples/. Stopwatch: tick-driven timer with lap recording. Quiz: multi-choice Q;&A with scoring. ColorPicker: 256-color palette browser. FileExplorer: Get-ChildItem browser with file details. Snake: playable game with tick-driven loop. Also adds -TickMs to Start-ElmProgram for time-driven demos, fixes Apply-ElmStyle char*int PS5.1 bug, and updates ConvertTo-AnsiOutput cursor tests.
close_reason: Four demos shipped (Snake removed). Also fixed Copy-ElmModel perf, Apply-ElmStyle PS5.1 compat, terminal driver input responsiveness, and stale-char rendering.

title: feat: add five example demos (Stopwatch, Quiz, ColorPicker, FileExplorer, Snake)
description: Add five new example scripts showcasing different capabilities of the Elm module. Stopwatch and Snake require the new -TickMs parameter on Start-ElmProgram for tick-driven state updates. Quiz and FileExplorer show multi-phase views and real PS integration. ColorPicker showcases dense 256-color grid rendering.
close_reason: Four demos shipped (Stopwatch, Quiz, ColorPicker, FileExplorer). Committed b304f2f.

title: bug: ComponentDemo no visual gap between Left and Right counters
description: Invoke-ComponentDemo uses New-ElmRow -Children @(left, right) with no spacer. The two boxes render immediately adjacent with no whitespace between them. Fix: add a spacer New-ElmText node between the two components in the Row.

title: fix: remove cursor hide/show from ConvertTo-AnsiOutput
description: ESC[?25l and ESC[?25h were emitted by ConvertTo-AnsiOutput on every full render, overriding the cursor hide set by Invoke-ElmEventLoop. Removed both sequences from the renderer; cursor visibility is now owned exclusively by the event loop try/finally block.
